{"success":true,"displayString":"const defineModel: <string, "bar" | "foo", string, string>(options?: (PropOptions<string, string> & DefineModelOptions<string, string, string>) | undefined) => ModelRef<...> (+3 overloads)","kind":"const","documentation":"Vue `<script setup>` compiler macro for declaring a
two-way binding prop that can be consumed via `v-model` from the parent
component. This will declare a prop with the same name and a corresponding
`update:propName` event.

If the first argument is a string, it will be used as the prop name;
Otherwise the prop name will default to "modelValue". In both cases, you
can also pass an additional object which will be used as the prop's options.

The returned ref behaves differently depending on whether the parent
provided the corresponding v-model props or not:
- If yes, the returned ref's value will always be in sync with the parent
  prop.
- If not, the returned ref will behave like a normal local ref."}

{"success":true,"displayString":"const defineModel: <number, string, number, number>(name: string, options?: (PropOptions<number, number> & DefineModelOptions<number, number, number>) | undefined) => ModelRef<...> (+3 overloads)","kind":"const","documentation":"Vue `<script setup>` compiler macro for declaring a
two-way binding prop that can be consumed via `v-model` from the parent
component. This will declare a prop with the same name and a corresponding
`update:propName` event.

If the first argument is a string, it will be used as the prop name;
Otherwise the prop name will default to "modelValue". In both cases, you
can also pass an additional object which will be used as the prop's options.

The returned ref behaves differently depending on whether the parent
provided the corresponding v-model props or not:
- If yes, the returned ref's value will always be in sync with the parent
  prop.
- If not, the returned ref will behave like a normal local ref."}

  prop.
- If not, the returned ref will behave like a normal local ref."}

two-way binding prop that can be consumed via `v-model` from the parent
component. This will declare a prop with the same name and a corresponding
`update:propName` event.

If the first argument is a string, it will be used as the prop name;
Otherwise the prop name will default to "modelValue". In both cases, you
can also pass an additional object which will be used as the prop's options.

The returned ref behaves differently depending on whether the parent
provided the corresponding v-model props or not:
- If yes, the returned ref's value will always be in sync with the parent
  prop.
- If not, the returned ref will behave like a normal local ref."}
